title: Dreadnaught cruiser
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Youmay|the [[New Sith Wars]] starship type known as the Dreadnaught cruiser|the ''[[Myrkos]]'', a [[Dreadnaught/Legends|dreadnaught]] [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]|linebreak=1}} {{Otheruses|Dreadnought}} {{StarshipClass |image= |name=Dreadnaught cruiser |designer= |manufacturer= |line= |model=Dreadnaught [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]]''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' |type=Cruiser |cost= |modifier= |sysmods= |length= |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed= |maneuverability= |engine= |hyperdrive=[[Hyperdrive/Legends|Equipped]] |hdrange= |hdsystem= |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen= |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament=[[Laser cannon/Legends|Laser cannons]] |complement= |bays= |escapepods= |crew= |skeleton= |passengers= |capacity= |cargohandling= |consumables= |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |availability= |role=[[Warship/Legends|Warship]] |firstuse= |retired= |affiliation=[[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] }} {{Quote|''[…]'' the Sith broke the ranks of their blockade to engage our fleet. With our capital ships drawing the focus of their cruisers and Dreadnaughts, we were able to send several gunships down to your aid.|Lord Valenthyne Farfalla, to Lord Hoth|}} The '''Dreadnaught cruiser''' was a type of [[Warship/Legends|warship]] used by the [[Sith fleet (New Sith Wars)|Sith fleet]] of the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] toward the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan|end]] of the [[New Sith Wars]]. In [[1001 BBY]], three Dreadnaught [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]] formed a part of a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] that [[First Battle of Ruusan|attacked]] a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] naval force in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]]. During the surprise attack, the Dreadnaughts targeted with their [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] two of the Republic's [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruisers]], whose [[Deflector shield/Legends|shielding]] had been compromised, although the Sith [[Starship/Legends|craft]] were forced to abandon their advance when their quarry deployed [[Aurek-class tactical strikefighter|starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] against them. Approximately a [[1000 BBY/Legends|year later]], the Sith used Dreadnaught cruisers to [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] Ruusan so as to prevent a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] fleet from landing reinforcements to aid an ongoing [[Ruusan campaign|ground campaign]] on the planet, and eventually the Dreadnaughts went on to engage the Jedi [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] in [[Space warfare/Legends|combat]]. ==Characteristics== The Dreadnaught [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]], which shared its name with the type of [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] known as the [[Dreadnaught/Legends|dreadnaught]],''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' was a type of [[Warship/Legends|warship]] equipped with [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] [[Engine/Legends|engines]] and armed with several forward-mounted [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]]. ==Role== Despite having less firepower, Dreadnaught cruisers could still pose a threat to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] 315-[[Meter/Legends|meters]]–long''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruisers]] once the latter's primary [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]] had been disabled. In turn, a sufficient number of Republic [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] could deter an advance by Dreadnaught cruisers. After using the Dreadnaughts in a surprise attack on a Republic [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] also utilized the [[Starship/Legends|vessels]] to enforce a [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] in order to prevent a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] fleet from landing reinforcements. ==History== ===The First Battle of Ruusan=== In [[1001 BBY]],''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' dates what ''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' describes as the [[First Battle of Ruusan]] to [[1001 BBY]]. as part of the [[New Sith Wars]],''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' the [[Sith fleet (New Sith Wars)|Sith navy]] of the Brotherhood of Darkness launched an [[First Battle of Ruusan|attack]] on a Galactic Republic fleet stationed in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] planet [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]]. The [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] force included the Brotherhood's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Sith Destroyer]] [[Nightfall (Sith Destroyer)|''Nightfall'']]; three Dreadnaught cruisers; two [[corsair battleship]]s; and multiple [[Interceptor/Legends|interceptor]] and [[Buzzard (starfighter)|Buzzard]] starfighters. The Republic fleet, consisting of four ''Hammerhead''-class cruisers and three [[Battleship/Legends|battleships]], had more starships with greater firepower, but [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Skere Kaan]] both used the element of surprise to the advantage of the Sith and coordinated the attacking force via the use of the [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] [[Force power/Legends|Force technique]]. [[File:HammerheadKnightErrant.jpg|thumb|right|300px|During the First Battle of Ruusan, Dreadnaught cruisers attacked Galactic Republic ''Hammerhead''-class cruisers (a vessel of the latter class pictured).]] The large Sith fleet—carrying out a dangerous maneuver for a force of such size—emerged from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] en masse, beginning to bombard the Republic vessels before they could even sound an alarm. When two of the Hammerhead cruisers lost their primary shields after suffering a strafing run by the Sith Buzzards, the Dreadnaught cruisers moved in to attack with devastating efficiency the suddenly-vulnerable capital ships. However, one of the Republic fleet's cruisers was carrying aboard a [[Unidentified Cerean Jedi|Jedi Master]] who was also proficient in the use of battle [[Meditation/Legends|meditation]]. After the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi]] began applying the technique to the Republic's benefit, the Hammerhead cruisers being targeted by the Dreadnaughts rapidly—and unexpectedly so to Kaan—deployed four full [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] of [[Aurek-class tactical strikefighter|''Aurek''-class tactical strikefighters]], which forced the Sith vessels to break off their attack run since they were coming under an assault by the Republic fighters. Ultimately, however, the Sith prevailed in the battle, in which the Republic fleet was completely destroyed. ===Maintaining a blockade=== {{Quote|The blockade is intact. The Jedi fleet still hovers just beyond our range.''"
"''Engage. ''[…]''''"
"''As you command, Lord Bane. We will engage the Jedi.|Admiral Adrianna Nyras and Darth Bane|}} [[File:Ruusan_TEA.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Dreadnaught cruisers were used by the Sith to blockade the planet Ruusan.]] Later, the Sith forces became involved in a protracted [[Ruusan campaign|ground campaign]] on Ruusan against the Jedi [[Army of Light/Legends|Army of Light]] [[Commanding officer/Legends|led]] by [[Jedi Lord|Lord]] [[Hoth (Jedi Lord)|Hoth]]. Subsequently, the Brotherhood of Darkness's navy established a planetary blockade in order to prevent a Jedi fleet mustered by Lord [[Valenthyne Farfalla]] from landing reinforcements to aid Hoth's efforts. The Sith fleet, which was commanded by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Adrianna Nyras]] and was nearly double the size of the newly arrived Jedi naval force, included Dreadnaught cruisers and other vessels. Since the Jedi fleet lacked the starfighters necessary to break the [[Troop transport/Legends|troop transports]] through the blockade, and since the relations between Hoth and Farfalla had become strained, the force did not engage the Sith fleet and simply waited outside the latter's targeting range. Around [[1000 BBY/Legends|one year after]]''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' establishes that the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness's]] [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] of [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] was broken shortly before the final [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]-[[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan|battle]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]]. the First Battle of Ruusan, however, Dark Lord of the Sith [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] ordered the Brotherhood's fleet to engage the assembled Jedi force. The Jedi capital ships were targeted by the Dreadnaught cruisers, and several Jedi [[Gunship/Legends|gunships]] used the opportunity provided by the Sith fleet breaking formation to finally land on Ruusan. Soon thereafter, the ensuing [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan|final battle]] on Ruusan saw the Jedi and the Republic emerge victorious in the overall war. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:DreadnaughtCruiserWookieepedia-CatAndMouse.png|thumb|right|300px|An excerpt from a Wookieepedia article on the Dreadnaught cruiser (bottom columns; in Aurebesh, transliteration available [[:File:DreadnaughtCruiserWookieepedia-CatAndMouse.png|here]]) was presented as in-universe text on a data screen in the ''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' episode "Cat and Mouse."]] The Dreadnaught cruiser was introduced in the [[2006]] novel ''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'', authored by [[Drew Karpyshyn]] as the first entry in the ''[[Star Wars: Darth Bane Trilogy]]''. In "[[Cat and Mouse]],"{{TCW|Cat and Mouse}} the [[March 26]], [[2010]] sixteenth episode of the [[Star Wars: The Clone Wars|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' television series']] [[The Clone Wars: Season Two|second season]],{{SWArchive|url=theclonewars/guide/episode216.html|text=''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide: Cat and Mouse}} a [[Datascreen/Legends|data screen]] aboard the Galactic Republic [[Venator-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Resolute/Legends|''Resolute'']] displayed tactical information on the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[Providence-class carrier/destroyer/Legends|dreadnaught]] [[Invincible/Legends|''Invincible'']]. The data on the screen also included, transliterated in the [[Aurebesh/Legends|Aurebesh]] script, an excerpt from an article on the Dreadnaught cruiser from the free-to-edit, unofficial online ''[[Star Wars]]'' encyclopedia known as [[Wookieepedia]].{{WookCite|url=Dreadnaught_cruiser?oldid=2927910|text=January 23, 2010 revision of the "Dreadnaught cruiser" article}} When asked about the [[canon]]icity of the said excerpt and the information contained therein, [[Lucasfilm Story Group]] member [[Matt Martin]] stated that he considered it an [[Easter egg (virtual)|Easter egg]], therefore implying that it was not canonical with respect to either the current ''Star Wars'' canon or the franchise's [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.{{Twitter|missingwords|status/1067139158987104256|[[Matt Martin]]|quote=@CharlyPyle I'd consider it more of an easter egg.|archivedate=20220227205451}} ==Appearances== *''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' {{Ab|Darth Bane: Path of Destruction (audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *{{TCW|Cat and Mouse}} {{Ncm}} {{C|Mentioned on [[Datascreen/Legends|data screen]]}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Crucero Acorazado Sith }} [[Category:Cruiser classes]] [[Category:Starship classes of the Brotherhood of Darkness]]
